summary: DeVault announces a webcast demonstrating the maintainer side of email-based patch review for git and mercurial. He notes that while git-send-email.io covers the contributor workflow, the maintainer perspective is underserved. The video shows him reviewing sourcehut patches and participating in discussions using mutt. He also plugs aerc, his own email client project designed as a mutt replacement.
key_points: Email-based patch review lacks accessible documentation for the maintainer side of the workflow
DeVault uses mutt as his primary tool for reviewing patches and participating in development discussions
He is building aerc as a modern replacement for mutt, tailored to this kind of workflow
money_quotes: I've been working on making the email-based workflow more understandable and accessible to the world.
One thing that's notably missing from this tutorial, however, is the maintainer side of the work.
key_insight: The email-based development workflow needs better documentation and tooling on the maintainer side to gain wider adoption.
